summary.intent: Add a resume button to each row in the sessions table that opens a terminal window running `claude resume <session_id>`, styled consistently with existing spawn buttons in SessionDetail.
summary.outcome: A `→` ActionIcon button was added to SessionRow next to the session ID, wired to a new `/api/sessions/:id/resume` endpoint that uses AppleScript to open Terminal. The live badge was repositioned to follow the arrow button for consistent column alignment.

repo: The Session type includes a `cwd` field, which is required to open Terminal in the correct directory.
Spawn/terminal buttons use Mantine ActionIcon with size `sm`, variant `light`, color `indigo` — this is the established pattern in SessionDetail around line 730.
The server uses AppleScript to open Terminal windows for spawning/resuming sessions.
New terminal actions require both a server endpoint (src/server.ts) and a corresponding API function (app/src/renderer/src/api.ts).

code.finding: Reference implementation for spawn button style: ActionIcon size=sm, variant=light, color=indigo
Resume button added next to short session ID; live badge repositioned after the arrow button to maintain column alignment
New POST /api/sessions/:id/resume endpoint added using AppleScript to open Terminal with `claude resume <session_id>`

workflow: Button placement relative to badges (like 'live') affects perceived column alignment — ordering matters for visual consistency.
Row-click propagation must be stopped on action buttons inside clickable table rows.

friction: SessionRow.tsx required multiple sequential edits (3 separate Edit calls) to get the layout right, suggesting the initial placement of the button and badge needed iterative adjustment.
Had to re-read SessionRow.tsx after initial edits because the file had been modified.
open_items: diff2html-theme.css appears in the modified files list but is not mentioned in the transcript — unclear if this was an intentional or accidental change worth reviewing.
